title: All Quiet on the Western Front
description: All Quiet on the Western Front is a anti-war novel by Erich Maria Remarque. Banned in Nazi Germany for being demoralizing and insulting to the Wehrmacht.
descriptionParagraphs: All Quiet on the Western Front is a anti-war novel by Erich Maria Remarque. Banned in Nazi Germany for being demoralizing and insulting to the Wehrmacht.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a anti-war novel, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Nazi Germany (1933-1945). The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned in Nazi Germany for being demoralizing and insulting to the Wehrmacht. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: All Quiet on the Western Front entered censorship debates as a anti-war novel associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.
